weather_report:
A cloudless dawn has ripened into a clean, open morning over Wembley; by now the sky is a calm, unbroken blue and the light draws crisp edges on rooftops. It feels dry and uncomplicated, visibility long and clear. The sun already has some bite, and with midsummer angles it will sharpen toward midday, so the UV will climb quickly even if the breeze feels gentle.

Expect a bright, straightforward day, especially through the late morning, with plenty of sunshine and a pleasantly warm touch that flatters short sleeves and light fabrics. Sunglasses and a dab of sunscreen will serve you well; bring a hat if you plan to linger. It’s a fine window for watering balcony plants, hanging fresh laundry, walking to the shops, or taking a late-morning coffee on a quiet bench in speckled shade. If you’re deskbound, crack a window and let the blue drift in, then steal a short stroll at lunch when the light is at its boldest.
